name: Flag Japan
desc: The Japanese national flag featuring a red circle on a white background.

name: Map of Japan
desc: A silhouette map of the Japanese island nation.

name: Japanese Castle
desc: A traditional Japanese castle like Himeji or Osaka Castle.

name: Shinto Shrine
desc: A Shinto shrine gate, iconic Japanese religious architecture.

name: Cherry Blossom
desc: Sakura, Japan's beloved cherry blossoms that bloom in spring.

name: Tanabata Tree
desc: The Tanabata bamboo tree used in Japanese star festival celebrations.

name: Sushi
desc: Sushi, one of Japan's most famous culinary exports worldwide.

name: Bento Box
desc: A Japanese bento box meal with rice, fish, and vegetables.

name: Steaming Bowl
desc: Ramen noodles in a steaming broth, iconic Japanese comfort food.

name: Rice Ball
desc: Onigiri, a Japanese rice ball wrapped in nori seaweed.

name: Rice Cracker
desc: Senbei, a traditional Japanese rice cracker snack.

name: Dumpling
desc: Gyoza, Japanese pan-fried dumplings filled with meat and vegetables.

name: Dango
desc: Dango, Japanese sweet rice dumplings on a skewer.

name: Teacup Without Handle
desc: Matcha green tea served during traditional Japanese tea ceremony.

name: Sake
desc: Sake, a traditional Japanese rice wine served in ceremonies.

name: Chopsticks
desc: Japanese chopsticks (hashi) used for eating sushi and ramen.

name: Japanese Dolls
desc: Hina dolls displayed during the Japanese Doll Festival.

name: Carp Streamer
desc: Koinobori, carp-shaped streamers flown for Children's Day in Japan.

name: Wind Chime
desc: Furin, Japanese glass wind chimes that ring in summer.

name: Tokyo Tower
desc: Tokyo Tower, the iconic red and white communications tower in Japan.

name: Snow-Capped Mountain
desc: Mount Fuji, Japan's tallest and most iconic mountain.

name: Hot Springs
desc: Japanese onsen, natural hot spring baths and ryokan experience.

name: Crossed Flags
desc: Crossed Japanese flags representing national celebrations.

name: Flower Playing Cards
desc: Hanafuda, traditional Japanese flower playing cards.

name: Fox
desc: The fox (kitsune) in Japanese folklore, messenger of Inari.

name: Dragon
desc: The Japanese dragon (ryu), a mythical creature in Japanese culture.

name: Kimono
desc: A traditional Japanese kimono worn during festivals and ceremonies.

name: Torii Gate
desc: The iconic red torii gate at Itsukushima Shrine in Miyajima.

name: School
desc: A Japanese school building representing Japan's education system.

name: Bullet Train
desc: The Japanese Shinkansen bullet train, famous for its speed and punctuality.

name: Motorcycle
desc: Japanese motorcycle brands like Honda, Yamaha, and Kawasaki.

name: Video Game
desc: Japanese video games from Nintendo, Sony, and Sega.

name: Cat Face
desc: Maneki-neko, the Japanese lucky beckoning cat.

name: Pine Decoration
desc: Kadomatsu, New Year's pine decoration in Japanese tradition.

name: Moon Viewing
desc: Tsukimi, the Japanese tradition of moon viewing in autumn.
